elements.sent-2411-001: Hypergraph learning with $p$-Laplacian regularization has attracted a lot of attention due to its flexibility in modeling higher-order relationships in data.
elements.sent-2411-002: This paper focuses on its fast numerical implementation, which is challenging due to the non-differentiability of the objective function and the non-uniqueness of the minimizer.
elements.sent-2411-003: We derive a hypergraph $p$-Laplacian equation from the subdifferential of the $p$-Laplacian regularization.
elements.sent-2411-004: A simplified equation that is mathematically well-posed and computationally efficient is proposed as an alternative.
elements.sent-2411-005: Numerical experiments verify that the simplified $p$-Laplacian equation suppresses spiky solutions in data interpolation and improves classification accuracy in semi-supervised learning.
elements.sent-2411-006: The remarkably low computational cost enables further applications.
elements.sent-2411-007: Over the past two decades, hypergraphs have become a valuable tool for data processing.
elements.sent-2411-008: It is defined as the generalization of a graph in which a hyperedge can connect more than two vertices.
elements.sent-2411-009: This allows hypergraphs to model higher-order relations involving multiple vertices in data, with applications in areas including image processing \cite{zhang2020hypergraph,shi2024continuum , bioinformatics \cite{klamt2009hypergraphs,patro2013predicting , social networks \cite{antelmi2021social,fazeny2023hypergraph , etc. In this paper, we focus on semi-supervised learning on undirected hypergraphs.}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Let $H=(V, E, W)$ be a hypergraph, where $V=\{x_i\}_{i=1 ^n$ denotes the vertex set, $E=\{e_k\}_{k=1 ^m$ is the hyperedge set, and $W=\{w_k\}_{k=1 ^m$ assigns positive weights for hyperedges.}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: We are given a subset of labeled vertices $\{x_i\}=:L\subset V$ and the associated labels $\{y_i\}\subset \mathbb{R}$.
elements.sent-2411-012: The goal is to assign labels for the remaining vertices $V\backslash L$ based on the training data $\{(x_i, y_i), x_i\in L, y_i\in \mathbb{R}\}$.
elements.sent-2411-013: We consider the standard approach that minimizes the constraint functional \begin{equation}F^{con}(u)= \begin{cases} F(u), \quad if u(x_i)=y_i,~ x_i\in L, +\infty,\quad otherwise. \end{cases}\end{equation} Here $F(u)$ is the regularization of $u: V\rightarrow\mathbb{R}$ that enforces the smoothness of $u$.
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}F^{con}(u)=  F(u), \quad if u(x_i)=y_i,~ x_i\in L, +\infty,\quad otherwise. \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-014: It implicitly assumes that vertices within the same hyperedge tend to have the same label.
elements.sent-2411-015: The constraint in $F^{con}$ ensures the minimizer of it to satisfy the given training data.
elements.sent-2411-016: One of the fundamental algorithms for hypergraph learning is the $p$-Laplacian regularization \cite{zhou2006learning \begin{equation}\label{eq:1.0} F_{CE}(u)=\sum_{k=1}^m w_k \sum_{x_i,x_j\in e_k}|u(x_i)-u(x_j)|^p,\end{equation} where $p>1$.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Notice that the hypergraph $H$ can be approximated by a weighted graph $G=(V,E_G,W_G)$ with clique expansion \cite{agarwal2006higher .}
elements.sent-2411-018: More precisely, for any $x_i,x_j\in e_k\subset E$, there exists an edge $e_{i,j}\in E_G$.
elements.sent-2411-019: The associated weight $w_{i,j =\frac{w_k {C_{|e_k| ^2}$, where $|e_k|$ denotes the cardinality of hyperedge $e_k$ and $C^2_{|e_k| =|e_k|(|e_k|-1)/2$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Then functional $F_{CE}$ is equivalent to applying the graph $p$-Laplacian \cite{el2016asymptotic \begin{equation}\label{eq:1.2} F_{G}(u)=\sum_{i,j=1}^n w_{i,j} |u(x_i)-u(x_j)|^p,\end{equation} to the weighted graph $G$.}
elements.sent-2411-021: It was shown that the approximation approach can not fully utilize the hypergraph structure \cite{agarwal2006higher .}
elements.sent-2411-022: Later in \cite{hein2013total , the authors proposed to overcome this limitation with a new hypergraph $p$-Laplacian regularization \begin{equation}\label{eq:1.1} F_H(u)=\sum_{k=1}^m w_k \max_{x_i,x_j\in e_k}|u(x_i)-u(x_j)|^p,\end{equation} which is deduced from the Lov\'{a sz extension of the hypergraph cut.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: In \cite{ikeda2023nonlinear , the authors defined the hypergraph $p$-Laplacian operator, which is multivalued, as the subdifferential $\partial F_H$.}

elements.sent-2411-034: Nevertheless, their high computational cost cannot be neglected for large-scale datasets and hinders further applications of the hypergraph $p$-Laplacian.
elements.sent-2411-035: The purpose of this paper is to provide an alternative to $F_H^{con}$ that can be uniquely and efficiently solved.
elements.sent-2411-036: We begin by addressing the non-uniqueness issue of the minimizer for $F_H^{con}$ and obtain a single-valued $p$-Laplacian operator from the subdifferential of $F_H^{con}$.
elements.sent-2411-037: The operator involves unknown parameters dependent on the structure of the hypergraph, preventing us from solving the associated $p$-Laplacian equations for semi-supervised learning.
elements.sent-2411-038: A simplified equation that disregards these parameters is then proposed as an approximation.
elements.sent-2411-039: It is mathematically well-posed: It admits a unique solution and satisfies the comparison principle.
elements.sent-2411-040: There exist hypergraphs on which the solution of the simplified equation coincides with a minimizer of $F_H^{con}$.
elements.sent-2411-041: Despite this simplification, we still refer to it as the hypergraph $p$-Laplacian equation.
elements.sent-2411-042: Through numerical experiments on one-dimensional data interpolation, we observe that the simplified hypergraph $p$-Laplacian equation substantially inherits the characteristic of $F_H^{con}$ that suppresses spiky solutions.
elements.sent-2411-043: Experimental results on real-world datasets indicate that it even improves the classification accuracy for semi-supervised learning.
elements.sent-2411-044: The most notable feature of the new equation is its low computational cost.
elements.sent-2411-045: When compared to the aforementioned algorithms, it dramatically reduces the computation time for semi-supervised learning on the selected UCI datasets from dozens of seconds to less than 0.5 seconds.
elements.sent-2411-046: Further applications of the hypergraph $p$-Laplacian for large-scale datasets become possible.
elements.sent-2411-047: This paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-048: In section 2, we establish a hypergraph $p$-Laplacian equation from the subdifferential of $F_H^{con}$ and propose a simplified version that is computationally feasible and efficient.
elements.sent-2411-049: The properties of solutions for the equation are also discussed.
elements.sent-2411-050: Numerical experiments are presented in section 3 to demonstrate the performance of the simplified equation for data interpolation and semi-supervised learning.
elements.sent-2411-051: We conclude this paper in section 4.

elements.algorithm-2411-001: \caption{Semi-supervised learning with hypergraph $p$-Laplacian.} \label{alg1} \begin{algorithmic} \REQUIRE A dataset represented by a hypergraph $H=(V,E,W)$, a training set $\{(x_i,y_i): x_i\in L\subset V, y_i\in\{1,2,\cdots l\}\}$. \FOR { $k=1:l$} \STATE Find the constraint: For any $x_i\in L$, let \begin{equation}u_k(x_i)=  1, if y_i=k, 0, otherwise. \end{equation} \STATE Interpolate $u_k$ on $V\backslash L$ by minimizing $F_{H}^{con}$ or solving equation \eqref{AE}. \ENDFOR \STATE For any $x_i\in V\backslash L$, let \begin{equation*} y_i=\arg\max_{1\leq k\leq l}u_k(x_i),\quad u(x_i)=y_i. \end{equation*} \RETURN $u$. \end{algorithmic}
